rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical aziridination of alkenes with ammonia to give NH-aziridines, with solvent-controlled chemoselectivity (MeOH favors aziridination over NH3 oxidation/N2 formation seen in DMF)
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the alkene (e.g., triphenylethene 1v) to its radical cation, followed by nucleophilic addition of NH3 and a second anodic oxidation of the resulting aminyl radical to a nitrogen-centered cation that closes to the aziridine; in MeOH, hydrogen-bond donation by solvent raises Eox(NH3), suppressing competing NH3 oxidation

key_intermediates.name: alkene radical cation (1v•+, triphenylethene radical cation)
β-ammonio carbon radical (Int2-a, NH3 adduct of radical cation, after deprotonation)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the alkene to a radical cation (Eox = 6.64 eV); NH3 nucleophilically adds to the less-substituted carbon (TS1-a, 8.7 kcal/mol), and the adduct is deprotonated to a neutral β-amino C-radical; a second anodic oxidation (Eox = 5.72 eV) gives a β-amino carbocation that undergoes near-barrierless ring closure to the aziridinium, which is deprotonated to the NH-aziridine. In DMF the competing pathway is direct anodic oxidation of NH3 to NH3•+ leading to N2/H2 formation.
rate_determining_step: Nucleophilic addition of NH3 to the alkene radical cation via TS1-a (ΔG‡ ≈ 8.7 kcal/mol in MeOH)

probe_reagents.name: triphenylethene (1v) as model substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Greatest contrast between MeOH (aziridination) and DMF (hydrogenation/N2 formation), used to probe solvent-dependent chemoselectivity

probe_reagents.name: Ammonia (NH3)
probe_reagents.observation: CV shows NH3 is oxidized preferentially in DMF but 1v is oxidized prior to NH3 in MeOH, indicating MeOH H-bonding stabilizes NH3 lone pair

notes: Two solvent-dependent pathways are computed: in MeOH, NH3 addition to 1v•+ (TS1-a, 8.7 kcal/mol) outcompetes endergonic NH3 oxidation (+17.9 kcal/mol), leading to aziridine; in DMF, NH3•+ formation is only +3.6 kcal/mol and leads to barrierless deprotonation/hydrazine/N2 formation (N2 hydrogenation pathway), explaining loss of aziridination selectivity. The MeOH selectivity is rationalized by Kamlet–Taft α (H-bond donor) parameter (α=0.98 vs 0 for DMF). Tetraphenyl aziridine 2u structure confirmed by X-ray.
